messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor 3.15 is Released!
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor 3.15 is Released!
messages.body_plain:
Congrats to the team on getting everything done. I did notice a handful of
the packages announced in release 3.15 don't seem to have made it though.
For a subset of new packages, links in the official release page take me to
Bioconductor 3.16 (devel) and they don't install using
BiocManager::install('pkgname', version = '3.15'). Obviously two I have a
vested interest in are in this group (extraChIPs and sSNAPPY) but there
seem to be a few others such as as APL, DifferentialRegulation, ompBAM,
terraTCGAdata, vectraPolarisData which are included in the announcement,
but can't be installed. I also noticed that this group were very late
package inclusions having their Github Contributions iIssues closed only 5
or so days ago. Have we missed something in our code, or is this just a
glitch that will resolve in the next day or so?

If it's any help, I did manage a successful install using
BiocManager::install('pkgname', version = '3.16')

Thanks again,

Steve

messages.body_plain:
Good afternoon,

See https://bioconductor.org/developers/release-schedule/

Wednesday April 20: Deadline to add new packages to the Bioc 3.15 manifest. Package submitted to tracker must have completed the review processes and been accepted to be added to the manifest.

You must have just missed the deadline. Your package sSNAPPY is 0.99.8 in the development branch, which would mean that it will be available from the release branch in six months' time.

--------------------------------------
Dario Strbenac
University of Sydney
Camperdown NSW 2050
Australia

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor 3.15 is Released!
messages.body_plain:
Thanks Lori. Really appreciate your efforts chasing this up.

All the best,

Steve

On Thu, 28 Apr 2022 at 21:37, Kern, Lori <Lori.Shepherd at roswellpark.org>
wrote:

> we investigated the issue and identified the cause.
> The recently accepted new packages should be available after the next
> build report posting.
> Sorry for the inconvenience
>
>

> before the deadlines (see
>
> https://secure-web.cisco.com/1ZElGk0dWxaMybf8b0q7Aa6bGn92fCTyEg2WiLaYE7xPX9_hqtULZ05jw5zwiwL23gyAmQEPXv6cmeiUNP97GfXM35NYvDg8Ujs2czImvxco-u9dX6rykplvPc--MKnJ09ZN64MAvJ5COMHdJnJznSvQvq0jH4H7jUaG9-eUEz5igETaZyEygkMHERiRyRXrQ5FMsQBOYDs6VRey3gJhZwI9gZGMtAVymVRcIUKNl16Nrwx-_as4540P5dCxZlrMal8wt8ZiAbZ_YR-tulUk8mhkXbxA5K8_-X2qysC5k8CN71VnMc-iamvtyPvdcHWTj/https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2FBioconductor%2FContributions%2Fissues%2F2556).

> someone check and explain please? Thanks!
>
>
> On 28/04/2022 04:37, Steve Pederson wrote:
> > Congrats to the team on getting everything done. I did notice a handful
> of
> > the packages announced in release 3.15 don't seem to have made it though.
> > For a subset of new packages, links in the official release page take me
> to
> > Bioconductor 3.16 (devel) and they don't install using
> > BiocManager::install('pkgname', version = '3.15'). Obviously two I have a
> > vested interest in are in this group (extraChIPs and sSNAPPY) but there
> > seem to be a few others such as as APL, DifferentialRegulation, ompBAM,
> > terraTCGAdata, vectraPolarisData which are included in the announcement,
> > but can't be installed. I also noticed that this group were very late
> > package inclusions having their Github Contributions iIssues closed only
> 5
> > or so days ago. Have we missed something in our code, or is this just a
> > glitch that will resolve in the next day or so?
> >
> > If it's any help, I did manage a successful install using
> > BiocManager::install('pkgname', version = '3.16')
> >
> > Thanks again,
> >
> > Steve
> >

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Minimize repo redundancy, is the original Github repo before package submissions still needed?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Minimize repo redundancy, is the original Github repo before package submissions still needed?
messages.body_plain:
Hello everybody, I have a quick question regarding best practises and
minimizing redundancy when it comes to Git repos after a successful
package acceptance.

For the Bioc submission, we created a new Git repo to start the process
(we had the code in another one before, but that was not supported for
the submission process). Now the package is accepted, and I am wondering
whether I actually still need the Git repo from my private account that
was used for the Bioc submission. Can I safely delete it, or is
Bioconductor at some point still needing it (for next release cycles?)?


Thanks,

Christian

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Minimize repo redundancy, is the original Github repo before package submissions still needed?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Minimize repo redundancy, is the original Github repo before package submissions still needed?
messages.body_plain:
Once accepted Bioconductor will use the code submitted, pushed, and updated at git.bioconductor.org.  any other private or personal repository can be managed (or deleted) as you are fit.

Get Outlook for iOS<https://aka.ms/o0ukef>

From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Christian Arnold <chrarnold at web.de>
Sent: Friday, April 29, 2022 6:18:29 AM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] Minimize repo redundancy, is the original Github repo before package submissions still needed?

Hello everybody, I have a quick question regarding best practises and
minimizing redundancy when it comes to Git repos after a successful
package acceptance.

For the Bioc submission, we created a new Git repo to start the process
(we had the code in another one before, but that was not supported for
the submission process). Now the package is accepted, and I am wondering
whether I actually still need the Git repo from my private account that
was used for the Bioc submission. Can I safely delete it, or is
Bioconductor at some point still needing it (for next release cycles?)?


Thanks,

Christian

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] SSH key_
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] SSH key_
messages.body_plain:
You need to activate and log into the GitCredentials account. https://git.bioconductor.org/BiocCredentials/login/?next=/BiocCredentials/
With the email shown above.
You can add keys there to control access. You're GitHub username (lyoussar) is already associated in the credentials account but you could add additionally keys there as well.



Get Outlook for iOS<https://aka.ms/o0ukef>
________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Loubna Youssar <lyoussar at gmail.com>
Sent: Wednesday, April 27, 2022 8:58:46 AM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] SSH key_

Dear maintenairs,

rifi package is hosted in github account as organization and unfortunately
I can not set up a key. You suggested to generate a key with my username
(lyoussar) and log with jens.georg at biologie.uni-freiburg.de (package
maintainer). Would that mean the package will be still hosted on the same
organization with a key generated from another account?

Thank you

Best,
Loubna
